title: WOS Assurance Declaration
description: Assurance-level and subject-continuity declarations (WOS Assurance Layer).

assuranceLevel.description: Ordered assurance level per S2.1. Base values: L1 (self-asserted), L2 (corroborated), L3 (verified), L4 (in-person). Implementations MAY define additional levels declared against the base four.

reference.description: The persistent subject identifier linking this instance to prior interactions. Enables a processor to re-identify the same legal person across workflow instances without a fresh assurance ceremony (Assurance S3.2).

scope.description: Continuity scope: how broadly this reference is trusted. 'instance': single workflow run. 'case': all runs under the same case identifier. 'tenant': all workflows within one agency tenant. 'deployment': entire WOS deployment.

disclosurePosture.description: Disclosure posture — independent of assuranceLevel per Invariant 6 (S4). 'anonymous': no identifying information. 'pseudonymous': token only. 'identified': real identity verified. 'public': public record.

subject.description: The entity about whom the attestation is made. Typically the subject reference URI or a WOS actor identifier for the claimant (Assurance S5.1).

predicate.description: The claim being asserted about the subject. Use a URI from a controlled vocabulary when possible (Assurance S5.2). Describes what property or capability is being attested.

basis.description: The evidentiary or procedural basis for this attestation. Describes how the claim was established: self-assertion, document verification, in-person check, or third-party corroboration (Assurance S5.3).

validityScope.description: Scope and duration for which this attestation is considered valid. When omitted, validity is implementation-defined. May be a time range, a workflow-phase identifier, or a case-level scope string.

^x-.$comment: Vendor extensions per Kernel spec §10.6 (extensions escape hatch)
